description_long: This object is a realistic broccoli head with a thick, pale green stalk and a dense, textured crown of tightly clustered florets. The stalk displays several branching segments, each supporting smaller offshoots that merge into the main crown. The crown itself is composed of numerous small, bumpy florets that are closely packed, giving the surface a rough and uneven appearance. The coloration transitions from a lighter green at the stalk to a more muted, yellow-green at the crown, with subtle variations in shading and texture. No leaves are visibly attached to the stalk or branches, and the overall form is asymmetrical, with the crown extending more prominently to one side. The surface finish is matte and rough, typical of fresh broccoli, and the object appears solid and compact.
description: A dense, asymmetrical broccoli head with a thick pale stalk and a tightly packed, rough-textured crown of small green florets. The surface is matte and uneven, with no visible leaves.
description_view_0: This view highlights the thick stalk and the branching structure leading up to the dense crown, showing the segmentation and texture of the stalk.
description_view_1: The crown is most prominent here, displaying the rough, bumpy surface of the florets and the overall asymmetrical shape.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the curvature and bulk of the crown, with the stalk partially obscured and the floret texture clearly visible.
description_view_3: A side profile showing the full length of the stalk and the branching pattern, with the crown extending outward and the segmentation of the stalk visible.
attribute_floret_clustering: Florets are densely packed across the crown, with minimal visible gaps and slight variation in floret size, creating a compact and continuous surface.
attribute_stalk_segmentation: The stalk is irregularly segmented with several visible nodes and branching points, but appears mostly continuous without clear section divisions.
attribute_branching_pattern: Branching is irregular and asymmetrical, with a few primary branches extending from the main stalk and supporting smaller offshoots that merge into the crown.
attribute_leaf_attachment: No leaves are visibly attached to the stalk or branches; the structure is bare with all visible mass composed of stalk and florets.
attribute_crown_density: The crown appears solid and highly dense, with tightly packed florets and no significant open areas or gaps.
